channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Israeli strikes kill four in southern Lebanon amid ceasefire talks
item.description: Drone attacks target vehicles in Nabatieh amid fragile ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the US.

item.title: Africa’s chances at the World Cup
item.description: There are six teams from sub-Saharan Africa to watch at this World Cup.

item.title: Robinhood slashes workforce by 10 percent amid restructuring
item.description: CEO Vlad Tenev said while the firm is in a strong financial position, it has been a ‘heavily-layered organization'.

item.title: Israel seizes power over occupied West Bank mosque from Palestinians
item.description: Hebron's mayor warns unilateral changes breach agreements, posing significant consequences for the region's stability.

item.title: Ronaldo’s last dance as Portugal face DR Congo in World Cup: All to know
item.description: Team news, prediction, and lineups as Cristiano Ronaldo’s Portugal begin quest for maiden Cup trophy against DR Congo.

item.title: Trump slams Israeli attacks on Lebanon at G7 summit
item.description: US President Donald Trump slammed Israeli attacks on Lebanon at the G7 Summit.

item.title: Qatar renews mediation efforts for regional stability after US-Iran deal
item.description: Qatar says it is working to prevent a return to war, following the signing of the MoU between the US and Iran.

item.title: Post-apartheid South Africa: 50 years after Soweto riots, what has changed?
item.description: Black students braved bullets to protest the discriminatory apartheid-era education policies.

item.title: The overnight World Cup?
item.description: For fans outside North America, watching the FIFA World Cup live means late nights and early mornings.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Day five
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Rahul Pathak has your FIFA World Cup 2026 day five recap.

item.title: Nearly all children globally exposed to at least one climate hazard: Report
item.description: Report highlights the growing threats posed by climate change and calls for the green transition to be accelerated.

item.title: Israel went to war with Iran, but Netanyahu may be the loser
item.description: Critics say PM Benjamin Netanyahu's Iran war strengthened Tehran, strained US ties, and left Israel weaker.

item.title: A Mexican citizen died in ICE custody. Now his family is demanding answers
item.description: Jose Guadalupe Ramos is one of 19 people who have died in ICE detention this year. Experts say he's part of a trend.

item.title: Optics of peace first, details later: The US-Iran 60-day challenge
item.description: Negotiators have yet to hold talks on key issues that have plagued US-Iran diplomacy for decades, say analysts.

item.title: Ukrainian drone detonates near Moscow
item.description: Footage shows the moment a Ukrainian drone hit a building under construction.

item.title: LIVE: France vs Senegal – World Cup 2026
item.description: Follow the build-up, analysis and live text commentary as France take on Senegal in a Group I clash in New Jersey.

item.title: From JCPOA exit to the 2026 deal: How US-Iran ties soured under Trump
item.description: Ties between the US and Iran have been strained through both of Donald Trump's terms as US president.

item.title: With Israel unleashed, there can be no peace in the Middle East
item.description: The US has long extended unconditional support for Israeli aggression. Now, it struggles to rein it in.

item.title: FBI says it foiled ‘planned attacks’ targeting White House UFC event
item.description: At least five people are in custody in an alleged plot that included 23 people, according to US media reports.

item.title: Gaza’s football fans follow World Cup matches despite hardships
item.description: Gaza’s football fans follow World Cup matches despite hardships.

item.title: Hardliners and moderates: What do Iran’s factions think of deal with US?
item.description: Factional divisions in Iran remain as leaders debate the implementation of deal with the US, amid fears of capitulation.

item.title: Brazil police arrest bungee jumping operators after woman dies
item.description: A woman in Brazil died after bungee jumping operators failed to attach her cord before a jump from a 40-metre bridge.

item.title: ‘Justice prevailed’: Is the Netherlands shifting its stance on Israel?
item.description: A court case, a Eurovision boycott and Gaza polling prompt Dutch debate over Palestine and Israel ties.

item.title: US visa rules leave Senegal’s World Cup fans behind
item.description: US visa rules leave Senegal’s World Cup fans behind

item.title: Lebanese rush back to their devastated homes in south after US-Iran deal
item.description: Thousands return to their homes following Iran-US deal despite Israel saying it won't end its occupation of Lebanon.
